title: Warm up
content: Three images depict taxi driver perspectives from inside vehicles in Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou. Each scene shows a different driving environment and local landmarks, setting up a comparison of city characteristics through a driver's viewpoint.
key_claims: Beijing scene shows heavy traffic and modern skyline with traditional architecture (Forbidden City visible)
Chongqing scene shows a driver reading a book with mountainous stilted architecture and cable car visible
Hangzhou scene shows a driver using a smartphone app with lake scenery and futuristic/AI technology graphics

title: An Evolving Nation
content: Title slide introducing the presentation theme 'A Ride Through China,' featuring images of a taxi sign and iconic architecture from different Chinese cities including the Forbidden City, a riverside city at night, and a traditional pagoda surrounded by trees.
key_claims: Presentation subtitle is 'A Ride Through China'
Visual imagery includes taxi signage and traditional/modern Chinese architecture

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! - Investigate and research
content: This slide introduces Session 1's learning framework using a 3-step process flow diagram alongside an isometric illustration of a modern city. The flow moves from identifying local features to development strategies to outcomes and positive impact.
key_claims: Learning process consists of three sequential steps: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact
Session focuses on investigation and research methodology for understanding city development

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: An interactive matching exercise slide where students connect three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to their corresponding development strategies. Lines show the correct matches between cities and strategies, accompanied by photos of each city's iconic landmarks.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Sustainable urban development
Chongqing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth
Hangzhou is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合)

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide reveals the correct city-strategy pairings using taxi driver imagery, correcting the previous matching exercise. It concludes with the overarching lesson that China is an evolving nation where each city tailors development strategies to its local characteristics, expressed by the Chinese idiom 因地制宜.
key_claims: Beijing's strategy is sustainable urban development
Chongqing's strategy is tourism & economic growth
Hangzhou's strategy is technology & tradition integration
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜 - adapting measures to local conditions)
